title: senecio salzmannii
intro: Senecio salzmannii, also known as the Mountain Arnold or Salzmann's ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, contributing to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. Senecio salzmannii is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and the risk of liver damage.
